codeReview.summary: All 9 newtypes (TaskId, AgentId, WorktreeId, BranchName, CommitSha, FeatureLeadId, CheckpointId, SessionId, SocketPath) are properly defined in ids.rs with correct derives, #[serde(transparent)], named accessors, Display, and FromStr. Re-exports are correctly added to lib.rs via `pub use ids::*;`. No From implementations exist between newtypes and inner types. cargo check -p swell-core passes.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature added `pub mod ids;` and `pub use ids::*;` to lib.rs for re-exports. This pattern wasn't explicitly documented in AGENTS.md but follows the same pattern used by other modules in the crate (e.g., `pub use audit::*;`, `pub use cost_tracking::*;`). The AGENTS.md section on 'Key Re-exports' doesn't explicitly enumerate what gets re-exported or how - just lists example re-exports. This is fine since the pattern is consistent with existing code.

summary: Feature pass: All 9 newtype wrappers are correctly implemented in ids.rs with proper derives, serde(transparent), accessors, Display, FromStr, and no From conversions. Re-exported via `pub use ids::*;` at crate root. String-backed types (BranchName, CommitSha, SocketPath) additionally provide `from_string()` constructor which is more ergonomic than requiring `from_str()` - this is a beneficial deviation from the spec which only mentioned from_str/as_str. No workspace-breaking issues in swell-core itself.
